title: Weak Model Escalation Posture
goal: Make delegation targets route by capability fit: weak targets escalate out of high-judgment work, and strong targets down-route mechanical work when a safe cheaper fit exists.
non_goals: Do not hard-code vendor-specific model names into shipped workflow policy.
Do not make local config an automatic scheduler.
Do not broaden model CLI evaluations into a full benchmark.

intent.outcome: Expose capability mismatch and overqualification as first-class delegation posture outcomes and evaluate them for model CLI targets.
intent.constraints: Keep policy model-neutral and local-posture-driven; weak targets may save tokens only when safe, and strong targets should not spend premium reasoning on safely delegable mechanical work.
intent.latitude: Tighten defaults/config payloads, docs, harness scoring, and smoke scenarios when they directly support capability-fit escalation or down-routing.
intent.escalation: Escalate before changing local-config schema semantics or adding vendor-specific scheduling rules.

execution.proof: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_model_cli_harness.py; make check

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Make weaker agents safer, make stronger reasoning more reusable, and reduce total cost without compromising quality.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Runtime/defaults payloads, docs, and model CLI harness checks now make weak-target escalation and strong-target down-routing visible.
